title: asclepias exaltata
intro: Asclepias exaltata, also known as the tall milkweed or exalted milkweed, is a perennial plant species in the milkweed family (Apocynaceae) native to eastern North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. Asclepias exaltata has been traditionally used to promote health benefits such as soothing coughs, reducing fever, and alleviating menstrual cramps, as well as to aid in the healing of wounds and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Asclepias exaltata include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by steeping the plant's leaves and roots in a solvent or by mixing the plant material with other herbs and oils.
